title: Ensure user auth tokens rotate within 90 days or less

input_row.title: Ensure user auth tokens rotate within 90 days or less
input_row.description: Auth tokens are authentication tokens generated by Oracle. You use auth tokens to authenticate with APIs that do not support the Oracle Cloud Infrastructure signature- based authentication. If the service requires an auth token, the service-specific documentation instructs you to generate one and how to use it.
input_row.rationale: It is important to secure and rotate an auth token every 90 days or less as it provides the same level of access to APIs that do not support the OCI signature-based authentication as the user associated to it.
input_row.audit: From Console: 1. Login to OCI Console. 2. Select Identity & Security from the Services menu. 3. Select Domains from the Identity menu. 4. For each domain listed, click on the name and select Users. 5. Click on an individual user under the Username heading. 6. Click on Auth Tokens in the lower left-hand corner of the page. 7. Ensure the date of the Auth Token under the Created column of the Customer Secret Key is no more than 90 days old.
input_row.remediation: From Console: 1. Login to OCI Console. 2. Select Identity & Security from the Services menu. 3. Select Domains from the Identity menu. 4. For each domain listed, click on the name and select Users. 5. Click on an individual user under the Username heading. 6. Click on Auth Tokens in the lower left-hand corner of the page. 7. Delete any auth token with a date older than 90 days under the Created column of the Customer Secret Keys.
input_row.additional_information: The Audit Procedure and Remediation Procedure for OCI IAM without Identity Domains can be found in the CIS OCI Foundation Benchmark 2.0.0 under the respective recommendations.
input_row.justification: The control pertains specifically to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) and its auth tokens, which are not applicable to AWS. AWS uses different authentication mechanisms such as IAM access keys, roles, and policies. Therefore, the control cannot be automated using AWS APIs or services like boto3, AWS CLI, or AWS Config, as these do not interact with OCI-specific resources.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. **Login to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Console:** - Navigate to the OCI Console and authenticate with appropriate credentials. 2. **Access Identity & Security:** - From the Services menu, select "Identity & Security." 3. **Select Domains:** - Under the Identity menu, choose "Domains." 4. **Review Users in Each Domain:** - For each domain listed, click on the domain name and select "Users." 5. **Check Auth Tokens:** - Click on an individual user under the "Username" heading. - In the lower left-hand corner, click on "Auth Tokens." 6. **Verify Token Age:** - Ensure the date of the Auth Token under the "Created" column is no more than 90 days old.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

---

**MANUAL STEPS:**

1. **Login to Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) Console:**  
   - Authenticate with appropriate administrator credentials.

2. **Access the Identity & Security Section:**  
   - In the OCI Console, click on the navigation menu.
   - Choose "Identity & Security".

3. **Select Domains:**  
   - Under "Identity", click "Domains".
   - For each listed domain, click the domain name.

4. **Review Users for Each Domain:**  
   - Inside the domain, select "Users".
   - For each user, click on the username to open the user detail page.

5. **Check Auth Tokens for Each User:**  
   - On the user detail page, in the lower left-hand navigation, click "Auth Tokens".

6. **Verify Age of Each Auth Token:**  
   - Review all listed Auth Tokens and their "Created" date.
   - Ensure no token's "Created" date is more than 90 days old.

7. **Determine Compliance:**  
   - The account is compliant if all Auth Tokens are less than or equal to 90 days old.
   - If any token exceeds 90 days, it is out of compliance and should be rotated (deleted and re-created).

---

**SOURCE:** CIS_Oracle_Cloud_Infrastructure_Foundations_Benchmark_v3_0_0
